title: US Senate prepares to vote on CLARITY Act
summary: The U.S. Senate Banking Committee is expected to announce a markup schedule for the CLARITY Act as early as tomorrow, according to Eleanor Terrett, host of Crypto in America. The committee has reportedly distributed a draft of the bill to some industry stakeholders ahead of the vote.While the bill's details are still under negotiation, further amendments may be made to reflect Democratic priorities. Terrett added that while the overall mood following a review of the bill and discussions with industry leaders has been positive, concerns remain that some provisions could alter key points previously thought to be settled.

title: Tom Lee: Three straight monthly gains would end BTC bear market
summary: The Bitcoin bear market is likely over, according to Tom Lee, Chairman of Bitmine (BMNR) and co-founder of Fundstrat. Speaking at the Consensus 2026 Miami event, he told CoinDesk that Bitcoin is breaking out of the downtrend that saw it fall from $126,000 last October to $60,000 in February. Lee noted that after positive monthly closes in March and April, the cryptocurrency has gained another 5% so far in May. He argued that Bitcoin has never recorded three consecutive monthly gains during a bear market, emphasizing that if BTC closes above $76,000 this month, the bear market will be definitively over.

title: Bitwise to acquire Superstate's USCC fund
summary: Crypto asset manager Bitwise announced it plans to acquire Superstate's USTB fund on June 1. Following the acquisition, the fund will be renamed the "Bitwise Crypto Carry Fund," while its existing Superstate blockchain infrastructure will be maintained. The fund, identified as USCC, currently holds approximately $267 million in assets under management.

title: Bitcoin OG Garrett Jin warns war risk remains as BTC nears $83K
summary: Bitcoin OG Garrett Jin has issued a strong warning to the market as BTC approaches $83,000, Blockbeat reported. Jin described the current U.S.-Iran ceasefire as a disguised peace, arguing that significant financial risks are accumulating beneath the surface. Despite strong earnings from Apple and AMD and Bitcoin's ongoing rally, he pointed to several signs that cracks in the credit market have already begun, including:- Banks increasing their loan loss provisions- Warren Buffett's record-high cash reserves- The bankruptcy of Spirit AirlinesHe advised investors to remain vigilant, suggesting that late May or the July earnings season could be a true day of reckoning for the market.
